# Preface {.unnumbered}

This case study applies a full-stack analytical pipeline to the UCI 130-US
Hospitals Diabetes dataset — a longitudinal collection of 101,766 inpatient
encounters from 130 US hospitals spanning 1999–2008. The outcome of interest
is **30-day hospital readmission**, a high-cost, partially preventable event
targeted by the 2010 Hospital Readmissions Reduction Program (HRRP).

The work is organized in two parts:

---

**Part 1 — Statistical Analysis** begins with rigorous exploratory data
analysis, causal inference (AIPW, IPTW, PSM) to estimate the effect of HbA1c
testing etc. on readmission, and a prospective experimental design including power
analysis for a model deployment trial.

**Part 2 — Machine Learning Evaluation** covers the full modeling pipeline:
four competing classifiers (Logistic Regression, XGBoost, LightGBM, TF/Keras),
hyperparameter optimization via Optuna, probability calibration, model selection,
holdout evaluation, SHAP-based interpretability, subgroup and fairness analysis,
and simulated production drift monitoring.

---

## Dataset

- **Source:** UCI Machine Learning Repository — Diabetes 130-US Hospitals (Strack et al., 2014)
- **Analytic sample:** 69,584 encounters after deduplication and exclusion criteria
- **Outcome:** 30-day hospital readmission (binary), ~7% positive rate
- **Period:** 1999–2008 across 130 US hospitals

## Pipeline Overview

| Stage | Tool | Output |
|---|---|---|
| **Raw data ingestion** | PySpark 3.5, Snowflake Spark Connector | Raw encounter table loaded to Snowflake (Google Cloud) |
| **Data warehousing & transformation** | Snowflake, dbt 1.11.7 | Deduplication, exclusion criteria, and imputation applied in the dbt marts layer |
| **Feature engineering & preprocessing** | Python, scikit-learn (`ColumnTransformer`, `OrdinalEncoder`, `StandardScaler`, `OneHotEncoder`) | Parquet train/validation/holdout splits + `preprocessor.joblib` to be used in val/holdout to transform raw features |
| **Exploratory data analysis** | R 4.3.2, tidyverse, kableExtra | Outcome distribution, covariate profiles, missingness analysis, Spearman and Cramer's V correlation matrices, etc. |
| **Causal inference** | R, WeightIt (AIPW, IPTW), MatchIt (PSM), sandwich estimators | Directional effect estimates for HbA1c, insulin change, and prior utilization; E-value sensitivity analysis |
| **Experimental design** | R, pwr | Group sequential design (O'Brien-Fleming), power analysis, etc. |
| **Model training — Logistic Regression** | scikit-learn `LogisticRegression`, Optuna, Elastic Net regularization | `lr_model.joblib` + Platt-calibrated probabilities |
| **Model training — XGBoost** | XGBoost, Optuna, scikit-learn `CalibratedClassifierCV` | `xgb_model.joblib` + calibrated probabilities; selected for deployment |
| **Model training — LightGBM** | LightGBM, Optuna, scikit-learn `CalibratedClassifierCV` | `lgbm_model.joblib` + calibrated probabilities |
| **Model training — Neural Network** | TensorFlow / Keras (multi-layer perceptron with cosine decay and dropout), Optuna, isotonic calibration | `tf_model.keras` + `tf_calibrator.joblib` |
| **Evaluation & model selection** | scikit-learn (AUC-ROC, AUC-PR, Brier score), matplotlib | Threshold sweep, calibration curves, train–validation gap analysis, holdout evaluation |
| **Interpretability** | SHAP (`TreeExplainer`, `DeepExplainer`), pandas, matplotlib | Mean \|SHAP\| rankings per model, cross-model directionality comparison, causal consistency check vs previous estimates, etc. |
| **Subgroup & fairness analysis** | pandas, scikit-learn | AUC-ROC and flag rate stratified by diagnosis group, age band, admission type, race, and gender; disparity ratios, etc. |
| **Drift monitoring** | Python, scipy, PSI (stability index) | 4-batch semi-annual simulation; KS statistic, PSI by feature, AUC and flag-rate trajectories across deployment periods, etc. |
| **Reproducible reporting** | Quarto 1.9, R Quarto book, Jupyter kernel, custom CSS | This combined HTML book hosted on GitHub Pages |

## Selected Results

- **Deployed model:** XGBoost (AUC-ROC 0.687 holdout, calibrated, τ = 0.12)
- **Flag rate:** ~15% of encounters flagged for intervention
- **Causal anchor:** HbA1c testing reduces readmission (AIPW RR ~0.92), consistent with SHAP directions and peer-reviewed literature
- **Drift:** No alerts across four simulated semi-annual deployment periods
